comments.text: For small fixed $r$, the functions $f(k,r)$ align with these following linear forms in $k$ that already appear in the OEIS: $f(k,3)=2k$ -> [A005843](https://oeis.org/A005843) (even numbers). $f(k,4)=\lfloor 3k/2 \rfloor$ ->[A032766](https://oeis.org/A032766) (numbers congruent to 0 or 1 mod 3, equivalently $\lfloor 3n/2\rfloor$). $f(k,5)=\lfloor 5k/4 \rfloor$ -> [A032769](https://oeis.org/A032769), essentially $\lfloor 5n/4\rfloor$ up to a periodic shift, so this is not a word for word match but I think a close match. $f(k,6)=k$ -> [A000027](https://oeis.org/A000027). These are possible connections rather than exact formulations of the extremal problem. I do think besides $r=5$ they could be exact (up to indexing). For $r=7$ and beyond, the conjectured asymptotic $(3/4+o(1))k$ does not yet appear to have a corresponding OEIS entry, or at least one that I could find. As a side note, from reading the OEIS links above it seems that the known values of $f(k,r)$ align with simple rational multiples of $k$ could suggest the general constants $c_r$ are rational and arise from clean modular set constructions.
